Nicotiana filifolia is a flowering plant species in the nightshade family, known for its tall, upright stems and clusters of small, white, pink, or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Nicotiana filifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Nicotiana filifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and alleviate respiratory issues.
